title: Prosthetic Rehabilitation of an Unemployed Mandibular  Ridge in Completely Edentulous Patient Using Neutral  Zone Technique: A Case Report
author: Manu Rathee1,
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The neutral zone technique is a method used in the fabrication of dentures where there is a highly atrophic ridge, neuromuscular in coordination, and a history of denture instability. The neutral zone is the potential denture space where the outward forces exerted by the tongue are neutralized by the inward forces of the cheeks and lips. Various materials such as impression compounds, tissue conditioners, waxes, and impression plaster have been used for recording the neutral zone. This case report describes in detail the steps in the fabrication of a mandibular denture using the low-fusing impression compound to record the neutral zone to improve the retention and stability of the lower denture.
